behaviors.description: Reject invalid `X-Signature-256` with 401 and do not forward body.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "signature does not match its body" and "body is not forwarded." 
Slot B: Failure boundary is HTTP handler + signature verifier + queue handoff gating. 
Slot C: A Unit test of only HMAC compare misses miswired handler flow that still enqueues on auth failure.
Integration-brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess; (b) no AI/ML output; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration.
Rejection-gate: Rejected System because behavior text only requires 401 + no forward, not real-network timeout/socket behavior.
Final label: Integration, driven by Slot B cross-module gate and Slot C miss-proof.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook route in-process with real middleware chain and an in-memory/persistent-test queue adapter; send a request with mismatched signature/body and assert HTTP 401 plus zero queue writes.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This test needs module wiring (route->auth->forward gate). A lower Unit test could pass while handler integration still forwards.

behaviors.description: Write event to persistent queue before returning HTTP 200.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment." 
Slot B: Ordering boundary between HTTP response path and persistence commit path. 
Slot C: Unit-testing enqueue call order without real persistence adapter can miss ack-before-commit bugs.
Integration-brake: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; local persistence wiring is enough; (b) no AI/ML; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration.
Rejection-gate: Rejected System because text does not require real external network/process; local persistent store transaction semantics are the target.
Final label: Integration, because Slot B is cross-module ordering and Slot C shows Unit blind spot.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real queue persistence module (test DB/file) with the HTTP handler; inject a controlled delay/failure point around queue commit and assert 200 is emitted only after durable write succeeds.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires interaction between handler and persistence layer; pure Unit cannot prove durable-before-ack ordering.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream event processing to finish.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing ... to finish." 
Slot B: Boundary between request handler completion and async worker execution lifecycle. 
Slot C: Unit-testing a promise helper misses real wiring where handler accidentally awaits worker pipeline.
Integration-brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess; (b) no AI/ML; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration.
Rejection-gate: Rejected System because behavior does not require real external infra, only non-blocking module interaction.
Final label: Integration due to Slot B async handoff boundary and Slot C await-miswire risk.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + a deliberately slow downstream consumer in-process; issue request and assert response is sent before slow consumer resolves, while event still processes asynchronously afterward.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real module handoff timing in app wiring; Unit doubles can hide accidental awaits.

behaviors.description: Respond within 3 seconds wall-clock despite slow concurrent downstream processing of earlier events.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "within 3 seconds wall-clock time" and "slow and concurrent." 
Slot B: Real runtime boundary: event loop scheduling, socket handling, and concurrent load behavior. 
Slot C: Integration with mocked clocks/clients can pass while real server under concurrency breaches SLA.
Rejection-gate: Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires wall-clock latency under concurrent load, which in-process fakes can hide.
Final label: System, anchored by Slot B real runtime effects and Slot C mock-hide risk.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual service process and send real HTTP traffic with concurrent slow background workload; measure end-to-end response latency and assert p95/individual response stays under 3 seconds.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The stated SLA is wall-clock under concurrency; lower levels cannot reliably surface runtime starvation/queuing effects.

behaviors.description: Do not write the same event ID to the queue more than once.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "ID was already received" and "not written ... a second time." 
Slot B: Boundary between dedupe key check and persistent queue write semantics (often unique/index + app logic). 
Slot C: Unit tests of ID-check logic miss persistence-level duplicate writes and race-adjacent wiring errors.
Integration-brake: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess; (b) no AI/ML; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration.
Rejection-gate: Rejected System because behavior text targets dedupe persistence behavior, not real network/process effects.
Final label: Integration since Slot B is storage handoff correctness and Slot C shows Unit insufficiency.
behaviors.test_description: Use handler with real queue persistence backend in test mode; send same event ID twice and assert first write succeeds, second is suppressed/rejected, and only one durable queue record exists.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires handler+storage interaction to catch duplicate-write failures; Unit logic checks are insufficient.

behaviors.description: If queue is full, return 503 and include `Retry-After` header.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "queue reports it is full" -> "returns 503 with a Retry-After header." 
Slot B: Boundary is error propagation/mapping from queue module to HTTP response formatter. 
Slot C: Unit-testing only status mapping can miss real adapter error type/metadata loss causing wrong header/status.
Integration-brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; (b) no AI/ML; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration.
Rejection-gate: Rejected System because no real network/subprocess failure mode is specified; mapping behavior is local module wiring.
Final label: Integration based on Slot B cross-module error contract and Slot C mock contract-gap risk.
behaviors.test_description: Run route with real queue adapter configured to signal capacity exhaustion; assert HTTP 503 and presence/value format of `Retry-After` in actual handler response.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs queue->HTTP contract wiring. Pure Unit can miss mismatched error objects and dropped headers.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow events.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit." 
Slot B: Boundary is queue configuration + persistence implementation enforcing capacity at write time. 
Slot C: Unit tests of counter logic miss real persistence behavior (transaction/order/reset) that allows overflow.
Integration-brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess beyond local persistence; (b) no AI/ML; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration.
Rejection-gate: Rejected System because behavior text does not require external network/process; local persistent backend interaction is sufficient.
Final label: Integration, justified by Slot B persistence enforcement boundary and Slot C lower-level blind spot.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue backend with small configured capacity; enqueue up to limit then one extra, asserting overflow is rejected and durable count never exceeds configured maximum.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Capacity enforcement is an implementation+storage interaction; Unit logic alone may not reflect true persisted limits.
